vulnerability.title: Configure opensc Smart Card Drivers
vulnerability.description: The OpenSC smart card tool can auto-detect smart card drivers; however, setting the smart card drivers in use by your organization helps to prevent users from using unauthorized smart cards. The default smart card driver for this profile is . To configure the OpenSC driver, edit the /etc/opensc.conf and add the following line into the file in the app default block, so it will look like: app default { ... card_drivers = ; }
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then var_smartcard_drivers='' OPENSC_TOOL="/usr/bin/opensc-tool" if [ -f "${OPENSC_TOOL}" ]; then ${OPENSC_TOOL} -S app:default:card_drivers:$var_smartcard_drivers fi else >&2 echo 'Remediation is not applicable, nothing was done' fi

triage_decision.reason: The remediation only edits /etc/opensc.conf to set the card_drivers parameter. This change does not affect system boot, SSH, or core authentication mechanisms and is reversible. Risk of lockout is minimal, making it safe to apply automatically.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Incorrect driver list could prevent legitimate smart card use

previous_review_verdicts.feedback:
Review output was not valid JSON. Raw: {
  "finding_id": "configure_opensc_card_drivers",
  "is_optimal": false,
  "approve": true,
  "feedback": "The plan will set the card_drivers parameter to an empty value in /etc/opensc.conf, which sa...

The plan will set the card_drivers parameter to an empty value in /etc/opensc.conf, which satisfies the requirement. However, the second sed command may add a duplicate line even when the parameter already exists, and there is no safeguard for missing app default block or backup of the original file.
concerns: The insertion sed command runs unconditionally and can create a duplicate 'card_drivers' line if the parameter already existed and was updated by the first command.
If the file does not contain an 'app default' block, neither command will make the required change.
No backup of /etc/opensc.conf is created before editing.
suggested_improvements: Add a check (e.g., grep) to verify whether 'card_drivers' already exists in the block before performing the insertion.
Create a backup of the original configuration file (e.g., cp /etc/opensc.conf /etc/opensc.conf.bak) before applying sed edits.
Consider consolidating the logic into a single idempotent command or using a configuration management tool to avoid duplicate entries.
